title: sphenotoma squarrosa
intro: Sphenotoma squarrosa, also known as the prickly tea tree, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and needle-like leaves. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and treating digestive issues. Medicinal preparations of Sphenotoma squarrosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address a range of health concerns. Additionally, the plant has been shown to have antiseptic and astringent properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional Australian herbal medicine.
